team.name: Chris R. Johnson
team.affiliation: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
titles: Founding Director, Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute
Co-Director, Center for Integrative Biomedical Computing
team.background: Chris Johnson is a faculty member within and founding director of the Scientific Computing and Imaging (SCI) Institute at the University of Utah where he is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science and holds faculty appointments in the Departments of Physics and Bioengineering. His research interests are in the areas of scientific computing and scientific visualization. With Professor Rob MacLeod, Dr. Johnson founded the SCI research group in 1992, which has since grown to become the SCI Institute employing over 200 faculty, staff and students. Professor Johnson directed SCI until 2018. Professor Johnson serves on several international journal editorial boards, as well as on advisory boards to several national and international research centers. Professor Johnson was awarded a Young Investigator's (FIRST) Award from the NIH in 1992, the NSF National Young Investigator (NYI) Award in 1994, and the NSF Presidential Faculty Fellow (PFF) award from President Clinton in 1995. In 1996 he received a DOE Computational Science Award and in 1997 received the Par Excellence Award from the University of Utah Alumni Association and the Presidential Teaching Scholar Award. In 1999, Professor Johnson was Awarded the Governor's Medal for Science and Technology from Governor Michael Leavitt. In 2003 he received the Distinguished Professor Award from the University of Utah. In 2004 he was elected a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering, 2005 he was elected a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science, in 2009 he was elected a Fellow of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics (SIAM) and received the Utah Cyber Pioneer Award. In 2010 Professor Johnson received the Rosenblatt Award from the University of Utah and the IEEE Visualization Career Award. In 2012 Professor Johnson received the IEEE CS Charles Babbage Award and in 2013 Professor Johnson received the IEEE Sidney Fernbach Award. In 2014, Professor Johnson was elected an IEEE Fellow and in 2019 he was inducted into the IEEE Visualization Academy. In 2020, Professor Johnson received the Leonardo Award.

team.name: Tushar M. Athawale
titles: Research Scientist, Visualization Group, Oak Ridge National Laboratory
Joint Faculty Assistant Professor, EECS, University of Tennessee, Knoxville
team.background: Tushar Athawale is a Research Scientist in the Visualization Group at Oak Ridge National Laboratory. He holds the position of Joint Faculty Assistant Professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) Department of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. He received M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in computer science from the University of Florida in 2014 and 2015, respectively. Before joining ORNL, he was Dr. Chris Johnson's postdoctoral fellow at the SCI Institute. He currently serves as the Associate Editor for the IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics journal and a program chair for the IEEE VIS 2026 conference.

team.name: Jixian Li
team.affiliation: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
titles: Postdoctoral Researcher, Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
team.background: Jixian Li is a postdoctoral research associate at the Scientific Computing and Imaging (SCI) Institute at the University of Utah, mentored by Dr. Chris Johnson. His work advances the integration of scientific visualization and machine learning, developing algorithms that help researchers extract and interpret detailed insights from computational fluid dynamics, medical imaging, and biomedical simulation data. He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Arizona under the supervision of Dr. Joshua A. Levine. His dissertation studied deep learning approaches for exploring collections of visual features of scalar fields. His research encompasses scientific visualization, uncertainty quantification, machine learning, and computational topology, all aimed at empowering scientists to compute, analyze, and communicate their discoveries more effectively.

team.name: Timbwaga A. J. Ouermi
team.affiliation: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
titles: Postdoctoral Researcher, Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
team.background: I am a postdoctoral research associate at the scientific computing and imaging SCI institute under the mentorship of Prof. Chris Johnson. I received my bachelor's degree in Physics and Computer Science from the University of Oregon in 2016 and my Ph.D. in Computing from the University of Utah in 2022. I was advised by Prof. Martin Berzins and Prof. Mike Kirby for my Ph.D. dissertation and by Prof. Hank Child for my undergraduate thesis.

team.name: Mengjiao Han
titles: Postdoctoral Researcher, Argonne Leadership Computing Facility, Argonne National Laboratory
team.background: Mengjiao Han is a Postdoctoral Researcher at the Argonne Leadership Computing Facility. She received her M.S. in Computer Science from the University of Delaware and joined Chris’s group in 2016 as a Ph.D. student. Her research focuses on scientific visualization using deep learning, with particular interests in flow field and uncertainty visualization, high-performance computing, and 3D Gaussian splatting for scientific data.

team.name: Nathan X. Marshak
team.affiliation: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
titles: PhD Candidate, Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
team.background: Nathan received his BSE in Computer Science in 2013 and MSE in Computer Graphics and Game Technology in 2014. His PhD dissertation focuses on numerical solutions to radiative transfer problems in cosmology.

team.name: Tark Patel
team.affiliation: Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
titles: PhD Student, Scientific Computing and Imaging Institute, University of Utah
team.background: Tark Patel is a Ph.D. student in the graphics and visualization track. He received his bachelor's degree in computer science at the University of Utah. His area of research is uncertainty visualization of diffusion MRI, particularly visualizing model uncertainty with glyphs. He develops methods for uncertainty quantification and mesh generation. Visualization techniques for this work leverage volume rendering and ray tracing.
